steps.connection: Make sure the device is powered on. THRM connects to BS1 automatically; BS2/BS3 models must first be connected over Bluetooth in system settings.
steps.release: Close other hardware tools that may be using the sensors or device connection.
steps.diagnostics: If retrying still fails, export a diagnostics package and include it with your report.

toasts.temperatureUnavailableDescription: Close other hardware monitoring tools and retry. The app will maintain a safe fan speed.

common.metaDescription: A third-party driver and control utility for the FlyDigi laptop cooler lineup.

bridgeWarning.default: CPU/GPU temperature reading failed. Try reinitializing temperature monitoring. If the CPU temperature is still empty, install or update PawnIO, or close other hardware monitoring tools.

coreService.unavailable: Core service is unavailable. Check whether THRM Core.exe was quarantined by security software, or restore/reinstall Core and try again.

history.title: Temperature and fan history
history.disabled: Background recording is disabled. Showing temporary history from this session only.
history.singleSampleCore: 1 background sample recorded. Waiting for more data.
history.singleSampleSession: 1 session sample recorded. Waiting for more data.

mode.smartDescription: Adjusts fan speed automatically based on real-time temperature
mode.manualDescription: You can adjust the mode and parameters in Settings

maxRpmHint.bs1: BS1 can reach up to 3000 RPM in overclock mode.
maxRpmHint.max4000: The overclock ceiling is unlocked. Maximum fan speed is 4000 RPM.
maxRpmHint.max3300: The current maximum is the strong gear at 3300 RPM. Use a PD 27W charger to unlock the upper limit.
maxRpmHint.max2760: The current maximum is the standard gear at 2760 RPM. Use a PD 27W charger to unlock the upper limit.
maxRpmHint.unknownCode: The device reported an unmapped maximum gear code: {{code}}
maxRpmHint.waiting: Waiting for the device to report maximum RPM capability.

hero.coreUnavailable: Core service is unavailable. Restore the background service first.

disconnected.title: Device not connected
disconnected.description: Please connect the cooler to your computer via Bluetooth

bridgeWarning.default: The temperature bridge failed to read data. Try reinitializing temperature monitoring and try again.
bridgeWarning.sharedProcess: This instance is reusing a shared process

aboutPanel.title: About {{name}}
aboutPanel.description: {{name}} is a third-party driver and control utility for FlyDigi laptop pressure-cooling devices.

version.noPrereleaseFound: No pre-release build is available right now.
version.checkFailed: Failed to check updates. Please try again later.
version.emptyReleaseNotes: No release notes available, or this fetch failed.
version.autoInstallHint: Downloads and installs silently and fully automatically, then restarts (only one UAC prompt; no manual install steps).
version.installingHint: Installing… the new version restarts automatically when finished.
version.installFailed: Failed to download or install the update: {{error}}
version.noInstallerHint: This release has no auto-installable package. Please download it from the release page.
version.floatDoneHint: This app will close and install automatically. A status window shows progress, then the new version restarts.
version.updaterWindowBody: Installing the new version automatically. Please do not close this window

items.question: Why does the CPU temperature show 0°C?
items.answer: When the CPU temperature shows 0°C, it usually means PawnIO communication was not established correctly. Reinstall the PawnIO components, restart the computer after installation, and then launch THRM again to confirm.

items.question: Which devices are currently supported?
items.answer: Currently supported models include FlyDigi BS1, BS2, BS2PRO, BS3, and BS3PRO.

items.question: What should I do if Bluetooth cannot find the device?
items.answer: If the device does not appear in the Bluetooth scan list, try long-pressing the cooler button to reset Bluetooth advertising. If it still does not recover, contact official support for further assistance.

credits.title: Acknowledgements
credits.description: Thanks to everyone who contributed code and to the sponsors who supported the project.
credits.error: Failed to load the acknowledgements list. Please check back later.
credits.thanks: Thank you for every bit of support — THRM is better because of you ❤

manualGear.title: Manual gear
manualGear.editHint: The 12 gears must increase from low to high, max {{max}} RPM

learning.title: Adaptive learning
learning.description: After longer runtime, the target RPM at each temperature point is fine-tuned automatically so real temperatures stay closer to the configured curve. Learned offsets can be reset at any time.
learning.targetDescription: Adaptive learning tries to settle steady-state temperature near this ceiling. Lower values favor cooling; higher values favor silence.
learning.offsetDescription: The main RPM corrections of the current learned curve relative to the base curve.
learning.noiseTestDescription: Measure real fan noise at each RPM with a microphone. Adaptive learning will then prioritize quiet optimizations where lowering RPM saves the most noise.

balanced.label: Balanced
balanced.description: Allows learned adjustments above and below the base curve.

cooling.label: Cooling first
cooling.description: Only allows learned RPM increases, avoiding higher idle temperatures.

quiet.label: Quiet first
quiet.description: Only allows learned RPM decreases, avoiding unnecessary fan ramp-up.

learning.predictiveDescription: Ramps up early from CPU/GPU power surges and temperature trends, before the measured temperature crosses a curve point. Independent from adaptive learning.

noiseTest.title: Fan noise test
noiseTest.description: Measure noise levels at different fan speeds with a microphone and build a noise profile to optimize adaptive learning.

intro.envTitle: Make sure the environment is quiet before testing
intro.point1: Turn off music, videos, air conditioning and other constant sound sources. Do not talk or type during the test.
intro.point2: Place the microphone about 20-30 cm from the cooler and keep it unobstructed.
intro.point3: If you use the laptop's built-in microphone, do not move the device during the test.
intro.point4: Results are relative noise levels (referenced to the quietest step) — no professional acoustic calibration is needed.
intro.sweepNotice: The test sweeps the fan from {{min}} RPM up to {{max}} RPM across {{steps}} measurement points and takes about 2-3 minutes. The fan will get noticeably loud; your previous settings are restored automatically afterwards.

setup.permissionError: Cannot access the microphone. Please check microphone permissions in system privacy settings ({{error}})
setup.stableHint: Background level is stable. You can start the test.
setup.unstableHint: Sound level fluctuations detected. Please keep quiet (no talking, no sound sources) before starting.
setup.deviceRequired: Device is not connected, fan speed cannot be controlled.

running.settling: Waiting for fan to settle at {{rpm}} RPM…
running.keepQuiet: Test in progress. Please keep the environment quiet and do not talk or move the microphone.

done.kneeValue: Noise rises sharply from about {{rpm}} RPM
done.resonance: Resonance peak detected near {{rpm}} RPM ({{db}} dB above trend). Consider setting a {{min}}-{{max}} RPM avoidance range.
done.lowConfidence: Total noise variation is too small for a reliable measurement. Move the microphone closer to the cooler, make sure the room is quiet, and run the test again. Saving this result is not recommended.
done.applyProfile: Save noise profile for adaptive learning

toast.cancelled: Noise test cancelled, fan settings restored
toast.restoreFailed: Failed to restore fan settings, please check the speed mode manually
toast.profileSaved: Noise profile saved. Adaptive learning will use measured noise for quiet optimization

avoidance.title: Noise avoidance range
avoidance.description: When smart control calculates a target RPM inside a noisy range, it automatically jumps outside that range to reduce intermittent bearing noise.
avoidance.minDescription: Lower RPM boundary of the noisy range.
avoidance.maxDescription: Upper RPM boundary of the noisy range.
avoidance.marginDescription: Extra RPM distance added when jumping outside the range.
avoidance.bypassDescription: Temporarily ignore the range above this temperature so cooling wins first.
avoidance.summary: Avoid {{min}} - {{max}} RPM with a {{margin}} RPM margin
avoidance.activeHint: Current target {{rpm}} RPM is inside the sensitive range and will be avoided automatically
avoidance.autoOnlyHint: Only applies in smart control mode
avoidance.hiddenHint: Hidden by default. These are generic default values that must be tuned for your device. Do not enable if you are unsure.
avoidance.confirmBody1: All parameters below are generic defaults. They only make sense after tuning against your device’s measured noise.
avoidance.confirmBody2: If you are unsure what you need, do not enable this to avoid harming normal cooling.

schedule.title: Time-based curves
schedule.description: Switch between different curve profiles by time period, useful for daytime performance and nighttime silence.
schedule.noRuleMatched: No rule matches the current time
schedule.autoControlHint: Only switches the active curve profile and does not force smart control on
schedule.empty: No schedule rules yet. Click the button above to add one.

hints.dragPoint: Drag the blue points to adjust RPM

history.detailsDescription: The curve page keeps a 1-hour history overview for temperature, power, and fan trends.
history.emptyEnabled: Background recording is enabled. Waiting for more temperature and fan samples.
history.emptyDisabled: Background recording is currently disabled. You can enable it on this page.
history.waitingMoreSamples: 1 sample recorded. Waiting for more data before drawing the trend chart.

profiles.title: Curve profiles
profiles.createDescription: Create a new profile based on the curve currently on the canvas. Names are limited to 6 characters.
profiles.manageDescription: Rename or delete existing curve profiles here.
profiles.deleteLastError: At least one curve profile must remain

importExport.title: Import / export curve profiles
importExport.description: Short strings can be copied and pasted
importExport.exportPlaceholder: Click Generate to show the export string
importExport.importPlaceholder: Paste a string starting with B2C1.

warning.title: Notice
warning.body: Speeds below 1000 RPM are below FlyDigi's official minimum design standard. Any issues caused by this must be borne by the user.

toast.saveFeatureFailed: Failed to save advanced fan settings
toast.learningResetDescription: The fan curve will now follow your configured values exactly

light.smartTempWarning: In Smart Temp mode the device controls lighting automatically, so color and brightness cannot be adjusted manually.
light.applyHintDisconnected: Will apply automatically on next connection

fan.autoControlDescription: Adjust fan speed automatically based on the temperature curve
fan.sampleSmoothingDescription: EMA weighting: larger values are smoother and steadier, smaller values respond faster. Keep the default if unsure.
fan.temperatureBaselineDescription: Choose the thermal source, GPU device, and specific sensor used for fan control. On multi-GPU laptops you can lock the dGPU here.
fan.processorDeviceHintDetected: The detected processor updates automatically with TempBridge.
fan.processorDeviceHintWaiting: CPU model not detected yet. It will appear automatically once TempBridge is available.
fan.temperatureSensorMultiHint: Select one or more cores; their average is used as the CPU control baseline. Select none for automatic.
fan.noCpuTemperatureData: No CPU temperature data is currently available
fan.gpuDeviceHintAuto: Auto mode will prefer the discrete GPU when possible.
fan.gpuDeviceHintLocked: Locked to the GPU device you selected.
fan.noGpuTemperatureData: No GPU temperature data is currently available
fan.currentControlSource: Fan control is currently using the {{source}} baseline at {{temperature}}°C.
fan.noControlTemperature: No valid control baseline temperature is available yet.
fan.transientSpikeFilterDescription: Ignore isolated temperature spikes so one-off jumps do not disturb smart control sampling and response.
fan.learningDescription: After longer runtime, the target RPM at each temperature point is fine-tuned automatically so real temperatures stay closer to the configured curve. Learned offsets can be reset at any time.
fan.temperatureHistoryDescription: When enabled, the core service keeps the last hour of CPU/GPU temperature curves. When disabled, temporary curves are drawn only while the GUI is open.
fan.curveProfileDescription: Switch fan control curves directly from settings without changing pages.
fan.customSpeedWarning: ⚠ Custom RPM disables smart temperature control

device.gearLightDescription: Control the gear indicator light on the device
device.powerOnStartDescription: Automatically run when the device receives power
device.smartStartStopDescription: Choose when cooling stops after the system shuts down

legionFnQ.enableDescription: Listen for Legion performance mode changes, including Fn+Q and Lenovo software switches.
legionFnQ.takeOverDescription: When a performance mode change is detected, switch the cooler to the mapped manual gear below.
legionFnQ.mappingDescription: The RPM level on the right is a low/medium/high sub-level within the same fan gear and affects actual RPM.

toasts.saveFailed: Failed to save Fn+Q plugin settings: {{error}}

system.themeDescription: Follow the system by default, lock to light/dark, or pick a custom theme from the install themes folder.
system.languageDescription: Change the app language instantly and remember the choice automatically.

hotkeys.title: Global hotkeys
hotkeys.description: Click a field on the right and press a key combination. It saves automatically on blur. Press Backspace/Delete or use the clear button to leave it empty and disable the hotkey.

manual.title: Cycle manual gear
manual.description: Cycle through Quiet, Standard, Strong, and Overclock while preserving each gear's sub-level memory.

auto.title: Toggle smart control
auto.description: Quickly switch smart temperature control on or off for game and quiet scenarios.

curve.title: Switch temperature curve
curve.description: Quickly rotate curve profiles for work, gaming, or night-time scenarios.

toasts.duplicate: The three hotkeys cannot use the same combination

system.autoStartTip: Running as administrator avoids a UAC prompt each time
system.reconnectDescription: Continue using the app configuration after reconnecting
system.reconnectTip: Recommended to prevent fallback to the device default mode after disconnects
system.blurDescription: Choose Acrylic, Mica, or Mica Alt. Changes take effect after restart.
system.suspendFanOffDescription: When the system sleeps or hibernates, set the fan to 0 RPM and turn off the gear light and RGB.

offline.message: Device is not connected. Some features are unavailable.

debug.pawnDescription: Temperature reading looks wrong? Try reinstalling the PawnIO driver.

toasts.uninstallWarning: PawnIO uninstall step returned a notice: {{warning}}

diagnostics.title: Export diagnostics
diagnostics.description: Bundle debug info, current config, and recent logs into a ZIP to share for troubleshooting.

bullets.disableAutoControl: • Smart temperature control will be disabled
bullets.fixedSpeed: • The fan will run at a fixed RPM

alerts.lightStripFailed: Failed to apply light strip settings: {{error}}

immediate.label: Immediate
immediate.description: Respond immediately to system load changes

delayed.label: Delayed
delayed.description: Delay response to avoid frequent start/stop cycles

smart_temp.label: Smart temperature
smart_temp.description: Switch lighting automatically based on temperature
